revision.comment: 「従六位」から別項化しました
revision.text:
'''大初位'''（だいしょい、だいそい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従八位]]または[[従九位]]の下、少初位の上の位階である。
[[律令制]]においては、さらに大初位上と大初位下の二階に分けられた。大初位は、一部の司の令史、[[大宰府]]の判事大令史、[[家司]]の一品家少書吏、二品家大書吏、職事一位家少書吏、[[掃部寮]]の少属などに相当する。
[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]においては上下の区別がなくされた。また、大初位は、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）[[8月22日 (旧暦)|8月22日]] <ref>内閣記録局『単行書・明治職官沿革表・職官部・一』（国立公文書館（ref.A07090183000））では、[[7月8日 (旧暦)|7月8日]]制定、[[8月20日 (旧暦)|8月20日]]改正とされている。</ref>に定められた職員令により、相当の職もなくなった<ref>[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/BIImgFrame.php?JP_NUM=40022968&VOL_NUM=00004&KOMA=168&ITYPE=0 法令全書「明治2年」]、国立国会図書館。</ref> 。
栄典としての位階制が定められた叙位条例（明治20年勅令第10号）、位階令（大正15年勅令第325号）には、初位はない。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://law.e-gov.go.jp/htmldata/T15/T15CO325.html 位階令] {{ja icon}} - 法令データ提供システム * [http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/BIImgFrame.php?JP_NUM=40022968&VOL_NUM=00004&KOMA=168&ITYPE=0 官位相当表] {{ja icon}} - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー
{{日本の位階}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいしよい}} [[Category:日本の位階|たいしよい]]

[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]においては上下の区別がなくされた。また、大初位は、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）[[8月22日 (旧暦)|8月22日]] <ref>内閣記録局『単行書・明治職官沿革表・職官部・一』（国立公文書館（ref.A07090183000））では、[[7月8日 (旧暦)|7月8日]]制定、[[8月20日 (旧暦)|8月20日]]改正とされている。</ref>に定められた職員令により、相当の職もなくなった<ref>[http://kindai.ndl.go.jp/BIImgFrame.php?JP_NUM=40022968&VOL_NUM=00004&KOMA=168&ITYPE=0 法令全書「明治2年」]、国立国会図書館。</ref> 。

'''大初位'''（だいしょい、だいそい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従八位]]または[[従九位]]の下、[[少初位]]の上の位階である。
[[律令制]]においては、さらに大初位上と大初位下の二階に分けられた。大初位は、一部の司の令史、[[大宰府]]の判事大令史、[[家司]]の一品家少書吏、二品家大書吏、職事一位家少書吏、[[掃部寮]]の少属などに相当する。
[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]においては上下の区別がなくされた。また、大初位は、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）[[8月22日 (旧暦)|8月22日]] <ref>内閣記録局『単行書・明治職官沿革表・職官部・一』（国立公文書館（ref.A07090183000））では、[[7月8日 (旧暦)|7月8日]]制定、[[8月20日 (旧暦)|8月20日]]改正とされている。</ref>に定められた職員令により、相当の職もなくなった<ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」]、国立国会図書館。</ref> 。
[[栄典]]としての位階制が定められた叙位条例（明治20年勅令第10号）、位階令（大正15年勅令第325号）には、初位はない。

[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]においては上下の区別がなくされた。また、大初位は、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）[[8月22日 (旧暦)|8月22日]]<ref group="注釈">内閣記録局『単行書・明治職官沿革表・職官部・一』（国立公文書館（ref.A07090183000））では、[[7月8日 (旧暦)|7月8日]]制定、[[8月20日 (旧暦)|8月20日]]改正とされている。</ref>に定められた職員令により、相当の職もなくなった<ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」]、国立国会図書館。</ref> 。
[[栄典]]としての位階制が定められた叙位条例（明治20年勅令第10号）、位階令（大正15年勅令第325号）には、初位はない。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
'''大初位'''（だいしょい、だいそい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従八位]]または[[従九位]]の下、[[少初位]]の上の位階である。
[[律令制]]においては、さらに大初位上と大初位下の二階に分けられた。大初位は、一部の司の令史、[[大宰府]]の判事大令史、[[家司]]の一品家少書吏、二品家大書吏、職事一位家少書吏、[[掃部寮]]の少属などに相当する。
[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官制]]においては上下の区別がなくされた。また、大初位は、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）[[8月22日 (旧暦)|8月22日]]<ref group="注釈">内閣記録局『単行書・明治職官沿革表・職官部・一』（国立公文書館（ref.A07090183000））では、[[7月8日 (旧暦)|7月8日]]制定、[[8月20日 (旧暦)|8月20日]]改正とされている。</ref>に定められた職員令により、相当の職もなくなった<ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」]、国立国会図書館。</ref> 。
[[栄典]]としての位階制が定められた叙位条例（明治20年勅令第10号）、位階令（大正15年勅令第325号）には、初位はない。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://elaws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - [[e-Gov法令検索]] * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー
{{日本の位階}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいしよい}} [[Category:位階制度]]

revision.text:
[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] '''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}}, and above {{ill|Minor Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Minor Initial Rank}} in the hierarchy.
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Old Calendar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Old Calendar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Old Calendar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Old Calendar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ_Unlinked]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] '''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}}, and above {{ill|Minor Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Minor Initial Rank}} in the hierarchy.
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Old Calendar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Old Calendar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.

{{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank}} {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}}, and above {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "2年" to "2nd year "
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank}} {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}}, and above {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869年8月22日}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.

{{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank}} {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}}, and above {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunar August20日}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.

In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.

{{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank}} {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank}}, and above {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|12=draft}} {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank|12=draft}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks of Japan|ja|位階|12=draft}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=draft}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=draft}}, and above {{ill|Lesser Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Lesser Initial Rank|12=draft}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-ranks/court-ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府|12=draft}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=draft}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=draft}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=draft}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=draft}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=draft}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=draft}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=draft}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this rank was included.
== Notes ==

revision.text:
'''大初位'''（だいしょい、だいそい）は、[[日本]]の[[位階]]における位の一つ。[[従八位]]または[[従九位]]の下、[[少初位]]の上の位階である。
== 概要 == [[律令制]]においては、さらに大初位上と大初位下の二階に分けられた。大初位は、一部の司の令史、[[大宰府]]の判事大令史、[[家司]]の一品家少書吏、二品家大書吏、職事一位家少書吏、[[掃部寮]]の少属などに相当する。
[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官 (明治時代)|太政官制]]においては上下の区別がなくされた。また、大初位は、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）[[8月22日 (旧暦)|8月22日]]<ref group="注釈">内閣記録局『単行書・明治職官沿革表・職官部・一』（国立公文書館（ref.A07090183000））では、[[7月8日 (旧暦)|7月8日]]制定、[[8月20日 (旧暦)|8月20日]]改正とされている。</ref>に定められた[[職員令 (太政官制)|職員令]]により、相当の職もなくなった<ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」]、国立国会図書館。</ref> 。
[[栄典]]としての位階制が定められた叙位条例（明治20年勅令第10号）、位階令（大正15年勅令第325号）には、初位はない。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/law/215IO0000000325 位階令] - [[e-Gov法令検索]] * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - 国立国会図書館近代デジタルライブラリー
{{日本の位階}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいしよい}} [[Category:位階制度]]

== 概要 == [[律令制]]においては、さらに大初位上と大初位下の二階に分けられた。大初位は、一部の司の令史、[[大宰府]]の判事大令史、[[家司]]の一品家少書吏、二品家大書吏、職事一位家少書吏、[[掃部寮]]の少属などに相当する。
[[明治時代]]初期の[[太政官 (明治時代)|太政官制]]においては上下の区別がなくされた。また、大初位は、[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）[[8月22日 (旧暦)|8月22日]]<ref group="注釈">内閣記録局『単行書・明治職官沿革表・職官部・一』（国立公文書館（ref.A07090183000））では、[[7月8日 (旧暦)|7月8日]]制定、[[8月20日 (旧暦)|8月20日]]改正とされている。</ref>に定められた[[職員令 (太政官制)|職員令]]により、相当の職もなくなった<ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2年」]、国立国会図書館。</ref> 。
[[栄典]]としての位階制が定められた叙位条例（明治20年勅令第10号）、位階令（大正15年勅令第325号）には、初位はない。

However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}

'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.

In the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>

In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

{{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}}

{{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}

'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.

In the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>

In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

{{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}}

{{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}

'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.

In the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>

In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

{{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}}

{{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}

'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.

In the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>

In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] to [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11433041]] Wikidata name: Greater Initial Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}}
{{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.
In the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.
== Notes ==

Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}

'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.

In the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>

In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>

== Overview ==

Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.

In the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>

In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{draft categories|↵}}" to ""
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11433041]] Wikidata name: Greater Initial Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}}
{{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]]'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.
In the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11433041]] Wikidata name: Greater Initial Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=These two {{ill|Greater Initial Rank|ja|大初位|1=Greater Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater Initial Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433041}}
{{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大初位]][[zh:大初位]]
{{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} are a bit weird to me as english translations go towards this form despite the fact that they do not use different words than the junior senior dichotomy. However using Greater/Lesser here to correspond to wikidata and translations I found in English[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 26 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Daishoi''' (大初位) is one of the {{ill|Court Ranks in Japan|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Court Ranks of Japan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is positioned below {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} or {{ill|Junior Ninth Rank|ja|従九位|1=Junior Ninth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Ninth Rank|qq=}}, and above {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|ja|少初位|1=Junior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Initial Rank|qq=}} in the hierarchy.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview ==
Under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, 大初位 was further divided into two sub-Ranks: 大初位上 (Daishoi-jō) and 大初位下 (Daishoi-ge). The Rank corresponded to positions such as the clerks of certain bureaus, {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}}'s 判事大令史 (senior clerks), 一品家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families), 二品家大書吏 (senior scribes for second-Rank families), 職事一位家少書吏 (junior scribes for first-Rank families in official roles), and the 少属 (junior assistants) of the {{ill|Kabu-ryō|ja|掃部寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabu-ryō|qq=}}.
In the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, distinctions between the upper and lower sub-Ranks were abolished. Furthermore, 大初位 lost its corresponding offices under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinryō|qq=}} of {{ill|August 22, 1869|ja|1869th year 8月22日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 22, 1869|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref group="notes">The Cabinet Records Bureau's "Independent Book: Meiji Official Ranks History, Section on Offices" (National Archives of Japan ref.A07090183000) states it was established on {{ill|July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar July8日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 8 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and revised on {{ill|August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja|Lunisolar August20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 20 (Lunisolar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.</ref><ref>[{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 法令全書「明治2nd year 」], National Diet Library.</ref>
In the honors system established by the {{ill|Joi Jōrei|ja|叙位条例|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joi Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Imperial Edict No. 10 of 1887) and later the {{ill|Ikai-ryō|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikai-ryō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=Court Ranks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}} (Imperial Edict No. 325 of 1926), no equivalent to this Rank was included.
